matter.title: Casco Bay Marine Infrastructure Group, Inc. v. Penobscot Narrows Terminal Authority LLC (accounting reconciliation)
matter.summary: Casco Bay Marine Infrastructure Group, Inc. sues Penobscot Narrows Terminal Authority LLC over the final project reconciliation, payment applications 1 through 47 on a $123456789.1 marine terminal contract: $6172839.46 of retainage, a $-2500.75 setoff, and a ledger that closes one cent out of balance.

plaintiff.organization_name: Casco Bay Marine Infrastructure Group, Inc.
plaintiff.full_name: Casco Bay Marine Infrastructure Group, Inc.

client.organization_name: Casco Bay Marine Infrastructure Group, Inc.
client.full_name: Casco Bay Marine Infrastructure Group, Inc.

fact_pattern.summary: Casco Bay Marine Infrastructure Group, Inc. sues Penobscot Narrows Terminal Authority LLC over the final project reconciliation, payment applications 1 through 47 on a $123456789.1 marine terminal contract: $6172839.46 of retainage, a $-2500.75 setoff, and a ledger that closes one cent out of balance.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Casco Bay Marine Infrastructure Group, Inc. completed a marine terminal for Penobscot Narrows Terminal Authority LLC under a contract valued at $123456789.1. At final project reconciliation, payment applications 1 through 47, the owner holds $6172839.46 in retainage and asserts a liquidated-damages setoff of $-2500.75 (a negative adjustment on the payment ledger).

The reconciliation also carries a change order priced at $0 (scope swap, no price change), a prompt-payment interest item of $1234.565 whose half-cent must round somewhere, and — after 47 payment applications — a ledger that closes $0.01 out of balance. Neither side's accounting software agrees on where the penny went.

Counsel Olivia B. Ellsworth of Kingsley & Associates will plead breach of contract and violation of Maine's prompt-payment act to release the retainage.
undisputed_facts: The owner holds $6172839.46 of retainage after final completion.
disputed_facts: Whether the $-2500.75 liquidated-damages setoff is enforceable.
The rounding of the $1234.565 interest item and the unreconciled $0.01.

exchanges.question: What is actually being held?
exchanges.answer: $6172839.46 — five percent of $123456789.1 — months after final completion.

exchanges.question: What is their justification?
exchanges.answer: A liquidated-damages backcharge of $-2500.75; we say the delay was owner-caused.

exchanges.question: Seriously, one cent?
exchanges.answer: Forty-seven pay apps and the ledgers close $0.01 apart. Their system rounds each line; ours rounds the total.

issues.issue: Release of retainage after final completion
sub_questions: Is retention past final completion a prompt-payment violation?

evidence.title: Payment application ledger
evidence.description: All 47 payment applications and the one-cent reconciliation gap.

amounts.label: Liquidated-damages setoff (owner backcharge)
amounts.basis: Negative adjustment asserted by the owner.

amounts.label: Unreconciled ledger difference
amounts.basis: One cent of drift across 47 payment applications.

facts.reconciliation_period: final project reconciliation, payment applications 1 through 47
